mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because moderate inflation reflects healthy aggregate nominal demand and supports corporate pricing power without triggering immediate monetary contraction.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between CPIAUCSL change and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of positive-change months.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because deteriorating labor income reduces aggregate consumption and forces negative earnings revisions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 achieves positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of trigger events when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or the mean forward return is non-negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) increase month-over-month while industrial production (INDPRO) decreases month-over-month, the S&P 500 posts negative 20-day forward returns because demand-supply divergence compresses corporate margins and signals a profitability slowdown.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month while INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when RSAFS increases and INDPRO decreases, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary policy, an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4 percent predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because a high risk-free rate increases discount rates and debt-service costs, outweighing any growth premium.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, or the mean return under this regime is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because stronger manufacturing output indicates positive near-term earnings surprises and improved capacity utilization.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO changes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below their trailing 12-month median predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because a slowdown in construction activity reduces downstream demand for building materials, durable goods, and household employment.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of trigger events when HOUST falls below its 12-month median, or the mean forward return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous three-month decrease in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because labor market deterioration overwhelms the valuation support from monetary easing.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of joint-signal months, or the mean 60-day return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansions because higher output directly maps to stronger aggregate demand and supports consensus revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of quarterly observations, or the rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year window.
